title: Document Review Findings
description: Structured output schema for document review persona agents

reviewer.description: Persona name that produced this output (e.g., 'coherence', 'feasibility', 'product-lens')

findings.description: List of document review findings. Empty array if no issues found.

title.description: Short, specific issue title. 10 words or fewer.

section.description: Document section where the issue appears (e.g., 'Requirements Trace', 'Implementation Unit 3', 'Overview')

why_it_matters.description: Impact statement -- not 'what is wrong' but 'what goes wrong if not addressed'

autofix_class.description: How this issue should be handled. auto = one clear correct fix that can be applied silently (terminology, formatting, cross-references, completeness corrections, additions mechanically implied by other content). present = requires individual user judgment.

finding_type.description: Whether the finding is a mistake in what the document says (error) or something the document forgot to say (omission). Errors are design tensions, contradictions, or incorrect statements. Omissions are missing mechanical steps, forgotten list entries, or absent details.

suggested_fix.description: Concrete fix text. Omit or null if no good fix is obvious -- a bad suggestion is worse than none.

confidence.description: Reviewer confidence in this finding, calibrated per persona

evidence.description: Quoted text from the document that supports this finding. At least 1 item.

residual_risks.description: Risks the reviewer noticed but could not confirm as findings (below confidence threshold)

deferred_questions.description: Questions that should be resolved in a later workflow stage (planning, implementation)

confidence_thresholds.suppress: Below 0.50 -- do not report. Finding is speculative noise.
confidence_thresholds.flag: 0.50-0.69 -- include only when the persona's calibration says the issue is actionable at that confidence.

severity_definitions.P0: Contradictions or gaps that would cause building the wrong thing. Must fix before proceeding.
severity_definitions.P1: Significant gap likely hit during planning or implementation. Should fix.
severity_definitions.P2: Moderate issue with meaningful downside. Fix if straightforward.

autofix_classes._principle: Autofix class is independent of severity. A P1 finding can be auto if the fix is obvious. The test: is there one clear correct fix, or does resolving this require judgment?
autofix_classes.auto: One clear correct fix -- applied silently. Includes both internal reconciliation (summary/detail mismatches, wrong counts, stale cross-references, terminology drift) and additions mechanically implied by other content (missing steps, unstated thresholds, completeness gaps where the correct content is obvious). Should include suggested_fix when possible.
autofix_classes.present: Requires individual user judgment -- strategic questions, design tradeoffs, or findings where reasonable people could disagree on the right action.

finding_types.error: Something the document says that is wrong -- contradictions, incorrect statements, design tensions, incoherent tradeoffs. These are mistakes in what exists.
finding_types.omission: Something the document forgot to say -- missing mechanical steps, absent list entries, undefined thresholds, forgotten cross-references. These are gaps in completeness.
